tossups.question: After being compared to a foaling horse in this novel, a woman screams during childbirth to “let them people know having a baby was more than a bowel movement.” In this novel, Darlene’s dress becomes stained with muscadine juice after Aunt Jimmy’s funeral. One character in this novel befriends China, Poland, and Miss Marie, three prostitutes who live above her storefront apartment. This novel’s chapters usually follow quotations from (*) Dick and Jane books. Marigolds fail to bloom in this novel, which Claudia and Frieda attribute to the incestuous rape of a character. A Shirley Temple mug inspires a girl to ask Soaphead Church for this novel’s title trait. For ten points, name this novel about the Pecola Breedlove, the first by Toni Morrison.
tossups.question_sanitized: After being compared to a foaling horse in this novel, a woman screams during childbirth to "let them people know having a baby was more than a bowel movement." In this novel, Darlene's dress becomes stained with muscadine juice after Aunt Jimmy's funeral. One character in this novel befriends China, Poland, and Miss Marie, three prostitutes who live above her storefront apartment. This novel's chapters usually follow quotations from (*) Dick and Jane books. Marigolds fail to bloom in this novel, which Claudia and Frieda attribute to the incestuous rape of a character. A Shirley Temple mug inspires a girl to ask Soaphead Church for this novel's title trait. For ten points, name this novel about the Pecola Breedlove, the first by Toni Morrison.

tossups.question: Note to players: this tossup is a mashup and one part of the mashup is meta.During this novel, one character asks for help obtaining a job with NYC Parks, but is instead used to kill a dog. One character in this novel dies during a meal of peach cobbler, during which he had started the ACF Nationals Cracker Barrel. In one scene in this novel, the main character lies on the ground after missing a question at an ICT side event before his friends plant marigolds in the hope that his baby will survive. After negging a tossup at ACF Nationals on (*) nacreous clouds, one character in this novel exclaims “Why did they write a tossup about my dad Cholly? He impregnated me at home while I was doing dishes?” For 10 points, identify this Toni Morrison novel about a Yale quizbowler and brother of Paul, who desires the title facial features.
tossups.answer_sanitized: The Bluest Eyesaac Kirk-Davidoff [accept The Bluest IKD]

He impregnated me at home while I was doing dishes?" For 10 points, identify this Toni Morrison novel about a Yale quizbowler and brother of Paul, who desires the title facial features.

tossups.question: Content warning: Rape. A character in this novel says she likes Jane Withers more than Shirley Temple, because Temple dances with Bojangles. This novel’s sections are titled after seasons, and it opens with three excerpts from a Dick and Jane book with first periods, then spaces removed. This novel is set in Lorain, Ohio, and its central character is blamed for killing Geraldine’s favourite cat. After seeing this novel’s protagonist scratching herself with her toe, the protagonist’s father (*) rapes her. The narrator of this novel, Claudia MacTeer, plants marigold seeds believing that will save the protagonist’s child, but the child dies and the protagonist subsequently descends into insanity. That insanity leads the protagonist of this novel to believe she acquired the title physical feature. Pecola Breedlove appears in, for 10 points, what novel by Toni Morrison?

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about China and Poland. For 10 points each:
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about China and Poland. For 10 points each:
parts: China and Poland live in the apartment above the Breedloves in this novel titled for a girl’s desired feature. Marigolds fail to bloom in this novel, mirroring the premature death of Pecola’s baby.

China and Poland are introduced in a chapter whose title is a string of words from these books about a white suburban family. These children’s books contain simple sentences such as “Look at Spot. See Spot run.”
This author of Beloved created China, Poland, and the Breedloves in The Bluest Eye.
parts_sanitized: China and Poland live in the apartment above the Breedloves in this novel titled for a girl's desired feature. Marigolds fail to bloom in this novel, mirroring the premature death of Pecola's baby.

China and Poland are introduced in a chapter whose title is a string of words from these books about a white suburban family. These children's books contain simple sentences such as "Look at Spot. See Spot run."
This author of Beloved created China, Poland, and the Breedloves in The Bluest Eye.
answers: Toni Morrison [or Chloe Anthony Wofford Morrison or Chloe Ardelia Wofford]
answers_sanitized: Toni Morrison [or Chloe Anthony Wofford Morrison or Chloe Ardelia Wofford]

bonuses.leadin: In one novel, this author described “men without skin” who push “the woman with my face” into the sea in a stream-of-consciousness description of the Middle Passage. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this author. Another novel by this author begins with a passage from “Dick and Jane” which is repeated without punctuation and then again without spaces.

Morrison used corrupted Dick and Jane quotes as chapter headings in this 1970 novel. At the end of this novel, Soaphead Church tricks Pecola Breedlove into poisoning a dog to convince her that she has the title feature.

Morrison also wrote Beloved, which is about an escaped one of these people who kills her baby before she is recaptured. Robert Hayden’s poem “Middle Passage” describes a journey these people were forced to undertake.
answers: slaves [or enslaved people; prompt on Africans or African-Americans or Black people]
answers_sanitized: slaves [or enslaved people; prompt on Africans or African-Americans or Black people]

Morrison also wrote Beloved, which is about an escaped one of these people who kills her baby before she is recaptured. Robert Hayden's poem "Middle Passage" describes a journey these people were forced to undertake.

bonuses.leadin: Mr. Soaphead gives a young girl this trait after she poisons a dog named Bob. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this trait. Claudia MacTeer dismembers a doll with this trait that resembles Shirley Temple, paralleling Cholly’s daughter’s destructive desire for this trait.
This novel by the author of The Bluest Eye chronicles the title ghost child’s arrival at 124 Bluestone Road, the home of former slave Sethe (“seth-uh”) and her daughter Denver.

This author of The Bluest Eye and Beloved became the first African American to win the Nobel Prize for Literature in 1993. Her most recent book, The Origin of Others, grapples with the American concept of race.
answers: The Bluest Eye [accept any answer mentioning blue eyes; prompt on a partial answer]
answers_sanitized: The Bluest Eye [accept any answer mentioning blue eyes; prompt on a partial answer]

bonuses.leadin: This novel begins in 1873 Cincinnati several years after Howard and Buglar run away. For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this Toni Morrison novel whose protagonist Sethe believes her house is haunted by a child she killed while enslaved.
In this other Morrison novel, Pecola believes Shirley Temple is pretty and she is ugly. This depressing novel’s narrator also tells of the abusive pasts of Pecola’s parents Cholly and Polly.
Morrison also wrote this novel that partially takes part in Michigan where Milkman becomes irritated because humans cannot fly. Later, Milkman tries to rob Pilate only to find rocks and a skeleton.

bonuses.leadin: In this novel, Paul D is described as having a “tobacco tin buried in his chest.” For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this novel in which the title ghost reappears to haunt the runaway slave Sethe in 124 Bluestone Road.
This American author of Beloved also wrote about a community called the Bottom which white people plan to transform into a golf course in her novel Sula.
In this novel by Toni Morrison, Pecola Breedlove, a woman who desires the title trait, is impregnated by her father Cholly. This work is partially narrated by Claudia MacTeer.

bonuses.leadin: This novel opens with a story about Dick and Jane, which progressively gets more and more compact until it’s unrecognizable. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this novel which ends when Claudia MacTeer, the narrator, attempts to grow marigold seeds, but they don’t sprout. In this book, Pecola goes insane after she’s raped by her father.
In this other novel, Paul D returns to the family of Sethe and Denver, re-awakening a ghost of the title character, who Sethe murdered on the Ohio River to prevent her from becoming a slave.
Both The Bluest Eye and Beloved were written by this contemporary author, the most recent Nobel Laureate in Literature from the US.
